mechanism_narrative: A high and rising federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative pressure on equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed was in a tightening cycle through 2005, raising rates from 1% in mid-2004 to over 4% by late 2005.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between the FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate over a three-month period is a reliable leading indicator of a recession, causing investors to de-risk portfolios and sell equities in anticipation of falling corporate profits.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in housing starts and retail sales growth signals a broad-based contraction in consumer activity, which is the primary driver of the US economy. This combination predicts lower corporate revenues and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending and housing are major components of GDP.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the combined signal does not remain negative and statistically significant over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing industrial production growth indicates weakening business investment and output. This effect is amplified during a monetary tightening cycle, as higher borrowing costs make capital expenditures less attractive, leading to lower forward earnings and stock prices.
failure_condition: The signal's negative performance is not statistically stronger when the 6-month change in FEDFUNDS is positive compared to when it is negative or flat.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls is a direct indicator of a robust economy, translating to higher aggregate income and corporate revenues, which supports positive equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment is a key coincident indicator used by the NBER to date business cycles.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following the top quintile of monthly PAYEMS changes is not significantly greater than the average return following the bottom quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP is the definition of an economic downturn. This confirmation of recessionary conditions causes a severe decline in investor confidence and earnings expectations, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following the release of a negative quarterly GDPC1 print is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation when the policy rate is already restrictive forces the central bank to tighten further than anticipated. This 'hawkish surprise' increases the risk-free rate and equity risk premium, causing a sharp repricing of equities to the downside.
failure_condition: The signal has a positive hit rate (predicts negative returns which are then positive) greater than 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpectedly strong retail sales report boosts near-term sentiment by signaling robust consumer health, leading to a short-term positive drift in equity prices as growth expectations are revised upwards.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return on days following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).
